is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study targets P. falciparum kelch 13 (k13) and P. falciparum multidrug resistance 1 (pfmdr1); enrolled patients had P. falciparum mono-infections.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports pfmdr1 codon 86 (N86Y) and k13 propeller codons including R539T, G538, R622I, etc. Pfmdr1:86 and multiple Pfk13 positions (539,538,622) are curated targets.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: Table 3 gives N86 n(%) per region per year (e.g. Kigoma 2016 118 (100.0)); Table 2 gives k13 mutant counts per region/year with numerators.

sub_country_location.evidence: Frequencies broken down by region (Kigoma, Morogoro, Pwani, Tanga, Mbeya, Mtwara, Tabora, Mwanza) and specific sentinel sites (Igombe, Ipinda, Yombo, Mkuzi, Mlimba, Nagaga, Simbo, Ujiji).

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Tables 2 and 3 report frequencies per individual year (2016, 2018, 2019, 2020, 2021), each within a single collection year.

african_field_samples.evidence: DBS samples collected from malaria patients at TES sentinel sites in Mainland Tanzania, 2016-2021.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary empirical study with PET-PCR confirmation and capillary sequencing of field samples.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Samples came from TES conducted by NMCP; '2016 study has been published [22], while the 2018 and 2019 studies are available online [25, 26]'; also part of PARMA network surveillance. Overlap with prior/related datasets likely.

supplement.evidence: Per-region, per-year frequency counts are provided in Tables 2 and 3 within the main paper; no supplementary tables required for extraction.

assessment.summary: Primary TES-based molecular surveillance study of P. falciparum drug-resistance markers (k13 and pfmdr1) across eight sentinel sites in Mainland Tanzania, 2016-2021. Reports per-region, per-year extractable frequencies including pfmdr1 N86 and several k13 codons. Meets all eligibility criteria but has high duplicate risk given prior/related TES publications and PARMA network context.
